task_classification.classification_reasoning: This task involves deciding on the best used car to purchase, which requires evaluating options and making a decision based on various criteria. The primary focus is on decision-making.

selected_framework.name: Pros and Cons Analysis
selected_framework.rationale: The Pros and Cons Analysis framework is well-suited for this task as it allows the user to systematically evaluate different car options based on the specified criteria (price, age, mileage, running costs) and make a balanced decision.

alternative_frameworks.name: RICE
alternative_frameworks.when_to_use_instead: If the user needs to prioritize and quantify the importance of different car features.

alternative_frameworks.name: SMART
alternative_frameworks.when_to_use_instead: If the user needs to set specific, measurable goals for the car purchase.

personality_adjustments_preview: High T (84%): Emphasis on quantifiable criteria and logical evaluation
High A (84%): Direct language, minimal hedging in recommendations
Moderate N (64%): Include some big-picture context around the car purchase
body.original_task_description: I am in Bromsgrove, UK and want to buy a small second hand car for no more than £6,000 that is not too old, reliable, has low mileage, and is cheap to run.
question_answers: Reliability, low mileage, cheap to run and maintain.
Mostly for short trips, but I will drive between Bromsgrove and London twice a month.
